speaker: SUB
text: Everyone says gold goes up when the dollar goes down, right?

speaker: MAIN
text: I pulled fifteen years of XAUUSD and DXY data. The correlation is minus zero point four two. That means fifty-eight percent of the time, they don't move opposite at all.

speaker: SUB
text: Wait, so the number one rule in gold trading is only right half the time?

speaker: MAIN
text: Less than half. And it gets worse. During 2022 and 2023, the correlation actually flipped positive for months. Dollar up, gold up.

speaker: SUB
text: But every analyst on YouTube says watch the DXY before trading gold!

speaker: MAIN
text: Let's look at what actually moves gold. Central bank buying hit thirteen hundred and thirty-six metric tons in 2024. That's the third straight record year. China alone added two hundred and twenty-five tons.

speaker: SUB
text: So governments are stacking gold like crazy?

speaker: MAIN
text: And here's the thing. When central banks buy, the DXY correlation breaks completely. Supply-demand overwhelms the macro signal.

speaker: SUB
text: OK so what about the other big one — gold is an inflation hedge?

speaker: MAIN
text: From 1980 to 2000, US inflation averaged three point two percent. Gold dropped from eight hundred and fifty to two hundred and sixty. Twenty years of inflation, seventy percent gold crash.

speaker: SUB
text: That completely kills the inflation hedge narrative.

speaker: MAIN
text: Gold hedges currency debasement, not consumer inflation. There's a massive difference. CPI going up three percent doesn't help gold. But M2 money supply expanding thirty percent in two years? That's 2020 to 2022, and gold went from fifteen hundred to two thousand.

speaker: SUB
text: So what's the real signal?

speaker: MAIN
text: Real interest rates. When you subtract CPI from the ten-year Treasury yield, that number has a minus zero point eight two correlation with gold since 2003. Way stronger than the DXY.

speaker: SUB
text: Minus zero point eight two? That's almost perfectly inverse!

speaker: MAIN
text: Real rates go negative, gold rips. Real rates go positive, gold struggles. It's that simple. The DXY trade is a cargo cult — people copy it because everyone else does, not because the data supports it.

speaker: SUB
text: So what's the takeaway for traders?

speaker: MAIN
text: Three rules from the data. One — watch real rates, not the dollar. Two — track central bank flows, especially China and emerging markets. Three — ignore CPI headlines. M2 expansion is your signal.

speaker: SUB
text: Fifteen years of data versus YouTube analysts. Data wins.

speaker: MAIN
text: Always does.

speaker: SUB
text: Next week we're testing another gold myth — does the London session open actually set the daily direction?

speaker: MAIN
text: Subscribe so you don't miss the data. We test trading myths every week.
